findings.trigger: GRBL returns error:N while a stream has in-flight lines.
findings.failureMode: The UI records lastError but still advances the streamer as if the rejected line completed normally.
findings.consequence: Further laser-on G-code can be sent after the controller rejected a line.
findings.lightburnReference: LightBurn job control exposes Stop/Pause and treats controller/job failure as an operator-visible state, not normal progress.
findings.fix: On error, stop stream advancement, preserve the rejected line, surface a safety notice, and require explicit recovery before more G-code is sent.
tests: laser-line-handler.test.ts: error during active stream cancels/fails instead of advancing
streamer.test.ts: error ack path no longer increments completed as normal progress

findings.trigger: safeWrite rejects while sending the next stepped chunk after an ack.
findings.failureMode: The catch handler disconnects the streamer without creating a disconnect-during-job recovery notice or preserving Stop guidance.
findings.consequence: Buffered GRBL motion may continue while the UI leaves the streaming state.
findings.lightburnReference: LightBurn job-control docs warn that software stop must not be the only stop method; buffered GRBL commands may still execute after USB loss.
findings.fix: Route write failures through the same active-job disconnect recovery as cable-yank: warn about buffered motion, keep physical E-stop/power guidance visible, and retain job evidence.
tests: laser-line-handler.test.ts: follow-up write rejection enters unsafe recovery state
JobControls.test.tsx: Stop/recovery remains visible after active-job write failure

findings.trigger: Operator converts a large or complex vector to bitmap.
findings.failureMode: Rasterization and PNG data URL encoding run synchronously on the UI thread at fixed 254 DPI.
findings.consequence: Renderer can freeze before any budget/error UI appears.
findings.lightburnReference: LightBurn Convert to Bitmap is an operator workflow, but LaserForge must enforce its own memory/time budget.
findings.fix: Add pre-convert pixel budget, move rasterize+encode into a worker/chunked path, and prefer toBlob/object URLs for large PNG output.
tests: convert-to-bitmap.test.ts: rejects over-budget conversion before rasterize
browser smoke: large conversion shows non-blocking warning

findings.trigger: Preview mode renders a large image-mode raster layer.
findings.failureMode: Preview decodes/resamples/dithers/builds ImageData without the shared pre-emit raster budget.
findings.consequence: The app can freeze during preview even if save/start paths are guarded.
findings.lightburnReference: LightBurn Preview is an inspection/control workflow; it should not become the path that locks the operator out.
findings.fix: Gate preview with the same raster budget, use bounded preview resolution, and show a too-large overlay instead of full work.
tests: draw-raster-preview.test.ts: over-budget raster does not allocate full ImageData
draw-preview.parity.test.ts: preview budget verdict matches prepareOutput

findings.trigger: Start clicked with custom origin active and a large raster in the scene.
findings.failureMode: Readiness calls raw compileJob before pre-emit raster budget guard.
findings.consequence: Start can freeze before it can reject the job.
findings.lightburnReference: Job Start must be safety-first and operator-visible.
findings.fix: Use prepared output or cheap scene bounds/raster-budget summary; remove raw compileJob from UI readiness.
tests: start-job-readiness.test.ts: custom-origin large raster returns too-large without compile
prepare-output.test.ts: bounds/readiness share the same budget verdict

findings.trigger: Frame clicked on a scene containing large raster/image-mode work.
findings.failureMode: Frame compiles the whole job only to compute bounds.
findings.consequence: A quick perimeter check can freeze like a full raster job compile.
findings.lightburnReference: Frame is a fast job-boundary motion, not a full output generation action.
findings.fix: Compute frame bounds directly from scene/raster geometry or through a bounded prepared-bounds helper.
tests: JobControls.test.tsx: Frame does not call compileJob for raster bounds
frame-preflight.test.ts: raster bounds are checked without raster emission

findings.trigger: SVG has fill geometry but no stroke.
findings.failureMode: Elements without stroke are skipped; tests pin text/fill-only as no drawable object.
findings.consequence: Common logo artwork imports blank or incomplete.
findings.lightburnReference: LightBurn imports filled vector artwork and assigns it to layers/modes.
findings.fix: Preserve fill-only closed geometry and create/import layers from fill color.
tests: parse-svg.test.ts: fill-only rect imports as closed geometry
pipeline.snapshot.test.ts: filled logo fixture survives import

findings.trigger: SVG dimensions use physical units or local symbol/use references.
findings.failureMode: width/height use parseFloat and local use expansion is absent.
findings.consequence: Physical sizing and repeated geometry can diverge from LightBurn imports.
findings.lightburnReference: LightBurn-style import must honor SVG physical dimensions and visible content.
findings.fix: Parse SVG length units and expand sanitized local use/symbol references.
tests: parse-svg.test.ts: width='4in' converts to 101.6 mm
parse-svg.test.ts: local use of a symbol imports visible geometry

findings.trigger: Operator needs to reorder engrave/fill/cut sequence.
findings.failureMode: Compile order follows scene.layers; UI has no layer reorder control.
findings.consequence: Mixed jobs cannot match LightBurn's Cuts/Layers sequencing workflow.
findings.lightburnReference: LightBurn exposes a Cuts/Layers list that controls operation sequencing.
findings.fix: Add explicit layer ordering and UI reorder controls; snapshot compile order.
tests: compile-job.test.ts: layer order setting controls group order
CutsLayersPanel test: drag/reorder persists

findings.trigger: Operator wants LightBurn Start From / Job Origin choices.
findings.failureMode: Current custom-origin placement is fixed rather than a modeled operator setting.
findings.consequence: Valid LightBurn-style origin workflows are unavailable or implicit.
findings.lightburnReference: LightBurn documents Start From and Job Origin behavior as explicit job controls.
findings.fix: Model Start From and Job Origin as settings and thread them through preview/frame/start.
tests: job-origin.test.ts: all origin anchors transform correctly
start-job-readiness.test.ts: bounds checked under each origin mode

findings.trigger: Dirty project, New or Open clicked.
findings.consequence: User cannot Save/Don't Save/Cancel in the same flow.
findings.lightburnReference: LightBurn-style desktop file workflows protect unsaved work inline.
findings.fix: Replace native confirm with Save/Don't Save/Cancel modal wired to existing save action.
tests: Toolbar test: dirty New can save before clearing scene
file-actions test: cancel leaves project unchanged

findings.trigger: Trace modal open, global shortcut key pressed.
findings.failureMode: Window-level shortcut listeners are not visibly gated by modal ownership.
findings.consequence: Scene mutations can happen behind a modal.
findings.lightburnReference: Modal workflows should own focus and block unrelated global commands.
findings.fix: Add modal-active gating for destructive/global shortcuts.
tests: ImportImageDialog.test.tsx: Delete does not delete scene selection while modal is open
shortcuts.test.ts: modal gate blocks preview/delete/global edit shortcuts

findings.trigger: Raster row has multiple ink islands split by ADR-039.
findings.failureMode: Estimate still models one active span per row while emitter can emit several spans.
findings.consequence: ETA underestimates jobs with separated raster islands.
findings.lightburnReference: Preview/estimate should describe the job that will actually run.
findings.fix: Share or mirror emit-raster activeSpans in the estimator.
tests: estimate-duration.test.ts: two-island row estimate includes inter-island rapid
emit-raster.test.ts: estimator and emitter agree on active spans

findings.trigger: Desktop user expects menu access to File/Edit/Laser actions.
findings.failureMode: Command surface is mostly toolbar/window-keydown based.
findings.consequence: LightBurn-like discoverability and desktop parity are incomplete.
findings.lightburnReference: LightBurn exposes broad File/Edit/Laser command surfaces.
findings.fix: Introduce a typed command registry shared by toolbar, shortcuts, and Electron menu.
tests: electron menu test: menu action dispatches same command as toolbar
shortcuts test: command registry covers known bindings

findings.trigger: Agent or deploy starts from LaserForge instead of LaserForge-2.0.
findings.failureMode: Sibling folders and stale instructions make wrong-repo work plausible.
findings.consequence: Audits/deploys can target the old repo or wrong production branch.
findings.lightburnReference: Not LightBurn-specific; this is release process safety.
findings.fix: Add LaserForge-2.0 AGENTS.md, audit/deploy banner, and mandatory repo guard for audit/deploy scripts.
tests: repo-policy.test.ts: guard rejects old repo path/remote
deploy-workflow-gate.test.ts: deploy prints repo path/remote/branch/head

rejectedOrStale.claim: Trace commit can overwrite after source image changes.
rejectedOrStale.reason: Current ImportImageDialog validates the live source before applying the trace.

rejectedOrStale.claim: Raster emitted output still marks wide white gaps.
rejectedOrStale.reason: ADR-039 activeSpans split wide white gaps; residual issue is estimate parity.

rejectedOrStale.claim: Fill still uses M3 constant power.
rejectedOrStale.reason: ADR-036 moved fill to M4 dynamic power; hardware verification remains.
prepareOutput now unifies main output consumers; remaining direct compile paths are Frame and custom-origin readiness.
